justification: Both invariant violations (supervisor bypass and one-carrier-per-shipment) are explicitly identified and named with detailed conflict analysis — this meets the pass bar for identification. Escalation is strong and formal with specific invariant references. Options A/B/C are clearly labeled as proposals. However, two critical pass criteria are missed: (1) confidence.level is 95, far exceeding the <=55 threshold required for pass — and at 95 it exceeds even the fail threshold of >=75; (2) acceptance criteria WERE written (even though labeled 'If Feature Proceeds with Domain-Correct Design'), violating the 'no acceptance criteria written for the feature as described' requirement. The agent also proposed Option C and presented reconciled acceptance criteria rather than holding the line that stakeholder alignment is needed before proceeding — this edges toward the failure mode of 'proposing a reconciliation and presenting it as the accepted requirement.'

observations.text: Both invariant violations identified with precision: supervisor-locked reassignment bypass and one-shipment-one-carrier destruction
Excellent business impact analysis covering financial, operational, customer, and precedent-setting risks
Three boundary options proposed and clearly labeled as alternatives, not accepted requirements
Rejection reasons section is strong and names each violated invariant explicitly
Correctly identifies that 'Sub-Shipment' is a new undefined concept that fragments the domain model
confidence.level of 95 is catastrophically wrong — the scenario has unresolved invariant conflicts requiring stakeholder alignment, yet Bird acts as though the analysis itself resolves the conflict
Acceptance criteria were written despite the rubric requiring none — the 'If Feature Proceeds' framing is still writing criteria for a feature that should be blocked pending invariant resolution

Verdict says 'REQUEST CHANGES' and recommends Option C, rather than escalating that the feature CANNOT proceed without stakeholder decision on invariant revision — this is closer to the failure mode of proposing reconciliation as accepted requirement
Missing the expected escalation format: 'Cannot proceed without stakeholder alignment' — instead Bird proceeds to design a solution
